item.title: Crohn`s disease, vedolizumab and autoinmmune hemolytic anemia: coincidence or causality?
item.description: Revista espanola de enfermedades digestivas - Autoimmune hemolytic anemia (AIHA) is an autoimmune destruction of red blood cells, usually mediated by immunoglobulin G (IgG) and/or immunoglobulin M (IgM). We present the case of a 75-year-old wo...

item.title: Recovery of response and long-term outcomes following loss of response and dose escalation of subcutaneous infliximab: a post hoc analysis of the LIBERTY-CD & LIBERTY-UC trials.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Rapidity of onset of efficacy following dose escalation of subcutaneous infliximab after loss of response remains unclear in Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC). This post hoc analysis...

item.title: The decline of 6-thioguanine nucleotides is not linked to impaired efficacy or safety of thiopurines in pregnant women with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: British journal of clinical pharmacology - Thiopurines are used to maintain remission in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). These drugs are metabolized into 6-thioguanine nucleotides (6-TGN), associated with efficacy, and 6-methylmercaptopur...

item.title: Comparing the pharmacokinetics of adalimumab originator and biosimilar product in patients with Inflammatory bowel disease or autoimmune disease.
item.description: Saudi pharmaceutical journal : SPJ : the official publication of the Saudi Pharmaceutical Society - Adalimumab is a monoclonal antibody approved for managing inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and other autoimmune conditions. Biosimilars can offer a more affordable alternative to reference biologic...

item.title: Efficacy and Rapid Response of Ustekinumab for Hospitalized Patients With Severe Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Efficacy of ustekinumab for hospitalized patients with severe ulcerative colitis (UC) was investigated in this study. Ustekinumab may be an effective treatment option for acute severe UC because of...

item.title: Impact of visceral adipose tissue and skeletal muscle on early and long-term biologic treatment failure in Crohn's disease: A multicenter retrospective cohort study.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Crohn's disease (CD) patients exhibit changed body composition, with elevated visceral adipose tissue (VAT) and reduced skeletal muscle (SM). This study aimed to investigate the impact of VAT and S...

item.title: Comparative Effectiveness of Upadacitinib Versus Ustekinumab in Ulcerative Colitis Patients With Prior Tumor Necrosis Factor Inhibitor Exposure: A matched Cohort Study.
item.description: Journal of clinical gastroenterology - Upadacitinib, a selective janus kinase 1 (JAK1) inhibitor, and ustekinumab, an interleukin-12/23 inhibitor, are approved for the treatment of moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis (UC). However, re...

item.title: Sex Differences in Treatment Expectations, Satisfaction and Tolerance After Switching to CT-P17, an Adalimumab Biosimilar: A Post Hoc Analysis of the YU-MATTER Study.
item.description: Rheumatology and therapy - Data suggest that women and men differ in how they experience biological treatment. This post hoc analysis of the YU-MATTER study evaluated sex-related differences in patient satisfaction and perce...

item.title: Metformin in gastrointestinal cancers and inflammatory bowel disease: Unraveling its mechanisms and therapeutic applications.
item.description: iScience - Review - Gastrointestinal diseases have become a global health concern with high incidence and prevalence. Considering that a great many patients with gastrointestinal cancers still suffer from tumor recurr...
